page: Amazon Web Services r5dn.metal is a memory-optimized bare metal server powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Additionally, it includes 3600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core and memory capabilities. The server achieves top-tier results in multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, including a Redis SET throughput of 76.05 million operations per second. It also demonstrates high throughput in static web serving and software compilation. However, single-core performance and single-threaded decompression tasks fall into the average range. Tradeoffs are evident in LLM text generation, where the lack of GPU acceleration results in weak to poor performance for medium and small models.

From a cost efficiency perspective, the r5dn.metal offers high resource density and massive memory capacity on a dedicated bare-metal footprint, making it highly efficient for workloads that can fully utilize its parallel processing and high-speed local storage. It is best suited for memory-intensive databases, high-throughput caching layers, enterprise applications, and data-intensive web serving workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services r5dn.metal is a memory-optimized bare metal server featuring 96 vCPUs, 48 physical Intel Xeon 8259CL cores, and 768.0 GB of DDR4 RAM. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it operates at 3.1 GHz and integrates 3600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage alongside a 100 Gbps network interface. Benchmark data highlights strong multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and excellent database execution speeds, including 76.05 million Redis SET operations per second. Conversely, single-core processing remains average, and LLM text generation performance is weak due to the absence of dedicated GPU hardware. This server represents a high-density, bare-metal resource profile optimized for memory-intensive enterprise applications, large-scale databases, high-throughput caching, and data-intensive web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5dn.metal is a memory-optimized bare metal server with 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR4 RAM, 3600 GB NVMe SSD storage, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, built for high-throughput database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r5dn.metal is a memory-optimized bare metal server featuring 96 vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and 3600 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A high-density bare metal platform featuring massive memory capacity, high-speed local NVMe storage, and 100 Gbps network throughput.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 768 GB DDR4 RAM for memory-intensive applications
3600 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfer
Strong multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Optimized for large-scale databases, caching layers, and enterprise workloads
